ocr.text: As part of Lockheed Martin's ongoing commitment to advancing satellite technology, the deployment of our latest satellite
constellation has been meticulously planned to enhance communication, surveillance, and navigation capabilities across North
America. The operational readiness of our CommunicationSatellites, currently numbering 10, plays a critical role in ensuring
seamless connectivity for both commercial and governmental applications. However, the SurveillanceSatellites, totaling 5, have
encountered deployment challenges that required an extensive evaluation of our launch strategies.

In addressing these issues, we have implemented various technical solutions, although some aspects remain under review, as
indicated by the operational status marked as TechnicalIssues. Moreover, the TotalCost(CAD) associated with these
SurveillanceSatellites has been projected at 3 million CAD, reflecting our investment in overcoming these hurdles.
Additionally, our commitment to mitigating EnvironmentalImpact is evidenced by our proactive CommunityOutreach
initiatives, which aim to engage local stakeholders in the deployment process. The feedback obtained from these initiatives has
been instrumental in guiding our InnovationProjects related to NavigationSatellites. Currently, these satellites are also
demonstrating promising operational metrics, with feedback scores expected to positively influence future projects.
Collectively, these metrics underscore Lockheed Martin's dedication to not only advancing aerospace technology but also
ensuring responsible and sustainable satellite operations in the region.
